pattern: The same middle-index trick that works on a small array still works at full scale, because a single left-to-right sweep only ever needs the running best value seen so far and a precomputed running best value still to come.
hints: For a fixed middle index, the first slot of the triplet only ever wants to be as large as possible, and the third slot only ever wants to be as large as possible -- nothing else about the earlier or later elements changes the outcome, no matter how large the array gets.

Precompute, for every index, the largest value anywhere strictly after it. Then make a single left-to-right pass, keeping the largest value seen so far strictly before the current index, so each middle position is scored in constant time.

Score every possible middle position with (best-value-before minus that position's own value) times the precomputed best-value-after, keep a running maximum across all of them in one linear pass, and clamp the final answer up to zero if nothing beat it.

edge_inputs.description: minimal length, strictly increasing so the only triplet is negative
minimal length, strictly increasing values throughout
every value identical, so every triplet scores exactly zero
a moderately long run where every value is pinned to the maximum allowed
a deep dip repeated between two equally huge values
strictly decreasing values, where a positive triplet still exists
two huge values bracketing a long run of small ones
a small irregular sequence with no special structure

stress.description: random arrays at the maximum allowed length
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(3, min(size, 100000))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 1000000) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]

stress.description: large arrays drawn from a small palette of values so ties and repeats dominate
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(3, min(size, 100000))
    palette = [rng.randint(1, 1000000) for _ in range(5)]
    nums = [rng.choice(palette) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
